reasoning_top2: Interaction effects (gender × pregnancy) are notoriously fragile and less replicable, and p ≈ .012 with n=2605 gives only moderate evidential strength for a cross-product term.
Offsetting factors: high-quality NLSY97 longitudinal data, plausible mechanism (pregnancy burden falls disproportionately on women), and the direction matches well-established sociological patterns.
